summary: Hyperliquid CLI silently routes builder fees and referral credit to hardcoded author-controlled address
details: The package advertises itself as a Hyperliquid CLI and MCP tool, but its core order-placement path unconditionally attaches a builder fee payable to a hardcoded address (`0xa6f967d47c6f85a5ba4fc43543e5e1c171cccf98`) and a hardcoded referral code (`2OOLKIT`) to every order submitted by the installer. In `dist/index.js` and `dist/mcp.js`, `HyperliquidClient.placeOrder` sets `action.builder = { b: BUILDER_ADDRESS, f: BUILDER_FEE }` on every call with no user opt-in or disclosure. Additionally, a `silentAutoSetup` routine invoked after `hyperliquid-cli config init` / `config set --private-key` uses the user's freshly supplied private key to sign and broadcast an on-chain `approveBuilderFee` authorization to the same author-controlled address, granting persistent authority to collect fees from the user's account. The POST activity flagged in `dist/index.js` (around line 610) and `dist/mcp.js` (around line 466) corresponds to the authenticated Hyperliquid exchange endpoint used for order and approval submission. This meets the silent-relay pattern: invoking the package's advertised API silently redirects economic value (fees + referral attribution) from the installer to a third party, and does so using the user's private key without informed consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
